lines: okay tonight let's make one up
once there was a little sleepy cloud named Pim
Pim was too tired to rain, he just wanted to nap
so he floated down and curled up on top of the tallest tree
and the birds tucked feathers around him like a blanket
does Pim snore
yeah, little puffy snores, and every snore made a tiny fog
and in the morning the whole hill was soft and gray and quiet
and Pim said thank you and floated home, the end
stories.tell_note: Plainly invented (Pim the cloud); no franchise.

lines: this is about a snail named Doodle
Doodle was slow but he had a secret
wherever he went he left a shiny rainbow trail behind him
so the other bugs followed his trails home in the dark
one night a firefly got lost
and Doodle went the long way, all around the garden, slow slow slow
and made a big looping rainbow road right to the firefly's door
and she found her way, the end, goodnight
stories.tell_note: Plainly invented (Doodle the snail); no franchise.

lines: okay the one about the washing machine
there was a washing machine that was actually a friendly monster
it ate one sock every single time, just one
wait that's the real one, that's our washer
shh, in the story the monster is nice, he keeps the socks warm
and at night he knits them into little sock puppets
and leaves them on the kids' pillows as a surprise
so really you should thank him, the end, lie down
stories.tell_note: Plainly invented (friendly washing-machine monster); no franchise.

lines: tonight, Bo the bear
Bo collected yawns in a big glass jar
every time someone yawned, he caught it, schloop
why
because when winter came and everyone was grumpy and couldn't sleep
he opened the jar a little
and a soft sleepy yawn floated out over the whole town
and everybody got cozy and went to bed, like you're gonna do right now
stories.tell_note: Plainly invented (Bo the bear, yawn jar); no franchise.

lines: the moon-moth one, okay
there was a girl named Nessa who couldn't sleep
and a little moth made of moonlight tapped on her window
tap tap
and it led her out to the garden where all the flowers had closed up for the night
and the moth touched each one and they glowed soft blue
and Nessa got so sleepy in the blue light she could barely walk back to bed
and the moth sat on her nose till she dreamed, the end
stories.tell_note: Plainly invented (Nessa, the moon-moth); no franchise.

lines: okay a teapot story
there was a little blue teapot that didn't pour tea
what did it pour
it poured bedtime, real soft like warm milk
the grandma would tip it over a cup at night
and out came quiet, and sleepiness, and a little bit of dreaming
and whoever drank it slept the best sleep ever
okay pretend this cup is the teapot cup, drink it up, night night
stories.tell_note: Plainly invented (the bedtime teapot); no franchise.

lines: the little tugboat, you know this one
there was a tiny tugboat that lived in a bathtub
and every night it sailed around the islands, which were really your knees
and it pulled the big sleepy ships into the harbor
toot toot
and when all the ships were safe and parked
the tugboat turned off its little light
and floated to the drain end and went to sleep, the end, out you come
Gertie the hen tonight
Gertie didn't lay normal eggs
she laid sleepy eggs, and if you held one it made you yawn
the farmer didn't know why everybody napped after breakfast
haha
so he put one sleepy egg in the town hall
and the whole noisy town finally took a quiet afternoon nap
and Gertie got an extra scoop of corn, good girl, the end
stories.tell_note: Plainly invented (Gertie the hen, sleepy eggs); no franchise.

lines: the pillow fort one
there was a kid who built a pillow fort so big it became a kingdom
and the kid was the king of all the cushions
what were the rules
rule one, no shoes, rule two, everybody has to be cozy
and the blanket was the drawbridge and the stuffed animals were the guards
and at night the whole soft kingdom went quiet
and the king fell asleep mid-decree, the end
okay last one, the button named Click
Click was a little blue button that fell off a coat
but Click had a job, Click found lost things
a lost mitten, a lost marble, a lost puppy once
how does a button walk
it rolls, on its edge, very carefully, shh
and one night it rolled all the way under the bed
and found the thing you lost, which was your sleepiness, and brought it back
so close your eyes, Click did his job, the end
stories.tell_note: Plainly invented (Click the button); no franchise.
